content.text: Memory MVP lesson for the NoMemoryVerifier component: Situation: ReadTimeRetrievalPlanner can incorrectly return need_memory=false for ambiguous recall questions. Action: run one short LLM verification call before accepting the no-memory plan; do not use hard-coded substring heuristics. Result: we avoid brittle lexical hacks while still catching recall intent. Lesson: for ambiguous no-memory recall decisions, prefer a cheap model-based verifier over substring matching.

content.text: Memory MVP lesson for the NoMemoryVerifier component: Situation: ReadTimeRetrievalPlanner can incorrectly return need_memory=false for ambiguous recall questions. Action: run one short LLM verification call before accepting the no-memory plan; do not use hard-coded substring heuristics. Result: we avoid brittle lexical hacks while still catching recall intent. Lesson: for ambiguous no-memory recall decisions, prefer a cheap model-based verifier over substring matching.

structured.fullText:
Understood. I’ll store this lesson for NoMemoryVerifier:

Use a cheap model-based verifier call for ambiguous recall cases before accepting `need_memory=false`, instead of brittle substring heuristics.
